forecasts.reasoning: Premier Danielle Smith explicitly stated on May 5-6 (1-2 days before knowledge cutoff) that any referendum would be held 'sometime in 2026,' making a July 1, 2025 announcement deadline virtually impossible. Multiple authoritative sources confirm this statement. The government introduced legislation lowering signature thresholds and extending collection periods from 90 to 120 days, indicating a deliberate, measured timeline toward 2026 rather than an expedited process for 2025.

Lesotho granted Starlink license on April 14, 2025 (very recent - only ~3 weeks before knowledge cutoff). Based on reference class of African countries: Mozambique took 8 months from approval (May 2022) to going live (March 2023), Zambia launched in October 2023 after approval, Zimbabwe took several months. While Starlink appears to be accelerating African expansion (multiple approvals in April-May 2025: Lesotho, Guinea-Bissau, DRC), the timeline is tight given only ~6-7 weeks from license grant to July 1 deadline. The map previously showed 'planned for 2025' but license was just granted. No evidence found of the map status changing to 'Available' yet. Given typical rollout timelines and the very recent license, probability is below 50%.

Based on comprehensive analysis of USDA FSIS recall processes and specific characteristics of the Cargill Kitchen Solutions Class III recall: (1) GAO data shows effectiveness checks average 38 days; applied to March 28 issue date suggests completion by April 10-15, 2025 (search_4_result_2); (2) This is a Class III recall (lowest risk, 4% of all USDA recalls per search_1_summary), which typically resolves faster; (3) Recall was voluntary and conducted "out of abundance of caution" with products that "do not pose health concern" (search_2_result_1); (4) 95-day window from March 28 to July 1 provides ample time; (5) No evidence of complications or enforcement actions. The 73% probability reflects high likelihood of closure before July 1, with residual uncertainty from inability to verify actual USDA page status as of May 7 knowledge cutoff and potential bureaucratic delays in final closure documentation.

Analysis of multiple sources through May 7 shows extremely low probability of a Trump-Xi phone call between May 12-June 15. Key evidence: 1) Trump explicitly stated "I would not call Chinese President Xi Jinping" in a Time interview published May 7 (search_1_result_0), eliminating one side of initiation. 2) China has consistently denied any calls occurred, with Foreign Ministry stating "China and the US have not engaged in consultations or negotiations regarding tariff issues" on April 28 (search_0_result_5), and refusing to arrange leader-level calls despite White House requests (CNN, April 11) (search_0_result_7). Since resolution requires BOTH sides acknowledge a call, China denying calls that Trump claims makes those calls ineligible. 3) April/May diplomatic developments show engagement shifted to official-level Geneva talks (announced May 7) rather than leader contact, indicating both sides prefer keeping leaders insulated. 4) China's position is that tariffs must be cancelled first before negotiations, which has not occurred. 5) March reports of June summit discussions appear to have been superseded by the Geneva diplomatic track and April's public denials. With Trump unwilling to initiate, China unwilling to acknowledge or arrange calls, and both sides actively engaging through lower-level channels instead, the probability is minimal.

There is definitive evidence that massive protests occurred in Turkey on May 1, 2025, with 286,584 participants across 78 provinces (Reuters, Al-Monitor). This easily exceeds the 10,000 participant threshold. Turkey has been in a sustained protest period since March 2025 following the arrest of Istanbul Mayor Imamoglu, with hundreds of thousands attending demonstrations and weekly protests planned. The Global Protest Tracker is designed to track "significant antigovernment protests" (Carnegie methodology), and May Day protests with nearly 300,000 participants in 78 provinces would qualify as "significant." Turkey is also listed in the question background with a Wikipedia link to "2025 Turkish protests," suggesting awareness of recent protest activity. Given the scale, official government acknowledgment, and sustained protest momentum, there's a very high likelihood (~75%) these protests will appear in the Tracker or similar protests will occur before July 1, 2025.
